architecture_fit.note: Two stacked attention layers with hand-set identity OV, no MLP — exactly the virtual-attention-head mechanism the goal names, a minimal delta from base_model.py.

baseline_comparison.note: Single-hop baseline (A2) clearly collapses in the peaked regime (0.22 @ alpha 0.1 vs full ~1.0); ablation strawmen add further contrast.

faithfulness.note: Clean causal ablations show both layers necessary (layer-1 ablation == single-hop baseline exactly), though on a hand-built circuit the result is partly forced by construction.

operating_range.note: Fidelity holds at ~1.0 across alpha 0.1–10.0 (two orders of magnitude), exactly where the single-hop shortcut degrades.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entire circuit is hand-set (E=I, W_V=I, W_O=I, given patterns) — the closed-form virtual-head construction written out by hand.

visual_judgement.note: Heatmaps (A1/A2/pred/truth/error) plus a single ablation line chart make both the reconstruction and the necessity of each layer immediately legible; defaults to latest run.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties axes (query×key, peakedness sweep), the single-hop baseline, and the ablation to the goal's exact claim.

notes: A clean, well-targeted attempt that answers the prior jury's 'trivial closed form' critique by reframing A2@A1 as the canonical virtual-attention-head circuit: two hand-set identity-OV attention layers writing the composed pattern into the residual stream, with no MLP. It reconstructs A_chain at ~1.0 fidelity across two orders of magnitude of alpha (composition_robustness ~1.0) while the single-hop baseline collapses, and adds causal ablations showing both layers are necessary. The only soft spot is that faithfulness rests on a hand-built circuit whose ablation outcomes follow by construction rather than from a learned model.
